\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Paraxial \doxylink{classGaussian}{Gaussian} beam illumination (homogeneous medium). 

Field model\+:    \[E(r) = \mathbf p\, \frac{w_0}{w(z)} \exp\!
\left(-\frac{\rho^2}{w^2(z)} + i k [\,Z + \tfrac{1}{2} C(Z)\,\rho^2\,] - i\,\zeta(Z)\right),
\] with standard definitions for beam radius $ w(z) $, curvature $ C(Z) $, Gouy phase $\zeta(Z)$, and cylindrical radius $\rho$ measured wrt the propagation axis. The magnetic field follows from $\mathbf H = (1/Z_m)\,\hat k \times \mathbf E$. 

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{Gaussian_8h_source_l00040}{40}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{Gaussian_8h_source}{Gaussian.\+h}}.
